body_txt: So Alice and Bob don't know each other yet, or Alice regards Bob with either indifference or dislike. But then, she witnesses him committing — or becomes the recipient of — some single act of kindness ( rescuing someone from bullies , stopping to help someone with errands, etc.) that makes her think "maybe he's not so bad after all." This causes her to develop a blossoming crush on him or otherwise shoehorns the two toward a romantic relationship. Psychology-wise, the trope works due to the belief on the part of the girl that only she has seen this " hidden side " of the guy, which makes her feel special and connected with him. After all, the appeal of the bad boy is that he only acts like a dick around people other than you . Savvy characters can exploit this. Essentially, a form of Relationship Upgrade that tends to happen very early on in the Romance Arc , if not start it entirely. Rescue Romance , Rescue Introduction , and Returning the Handkerchief are particularly common types of Samaritan Relationship Starter. A related trope is Single Woman Seeks Good Man , where one character is attracted to another because they're usually like this. Opposite of Turned Off by the Jerkass , where a character dumps or rejects someone because of how awful they are to other people. Often overlaps with Because You Were Nice to Me .
